raw_result.description: AI-powered academic search engine and API for searching peer-reviewed research papers and evidence-based answers.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs show REST authentication via x-api-key header. Official MCP docs describe OAuth for agent clients such as ChatGPT and Claude, and bearer-token/API-key based enterprise MCP access.
raw_result.gating_reason: Consensus offers a public Free plan and publicly accessible API/MCP documentation; paid plans expand usage and API access, while enterprise offers higher limits and custom pricing.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST Search API with documented quick_search endpoint(s) plus an official MCP server exposing agent tools such as search and fetch.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP documentation is published, with server URL https://mcp.consensus.app/mcp and setup guidance for ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, and others.

raw_result.blocker: Although public docs exist, self-serve API usage appears tied to paid Team/Enterprise access and plan-based limits rather than clearly available free self-serve API credentials for all users.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Pricing page states plan-based limits (for example Free monthly search limits) and Team API access is marked rate limited at $0.10/request; MCP docs note HTTP 429 when limits are exceeded and Enterprise offers higher/custom limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Consensus has first-party API reference docs, a dedicated API landing page surfaced during browsing, and an official MCP server with native agent-client setup docs, which are strong signals for agent toolkit potential.
